main_turf.bias_notes: Clockwise. A magnificent, expansive track that is universally considered one of the fairest in New South Wales. The sweeping turns and long 400m straight ensure that every horse gets their chance, whether leading or swooping from the back.
main_turf.post_bias: Because of the wide, sweeping nature of the track and long runs to the first turn in most distances (especially the 1100m and 1300m chutes), outside barrier draws are far less penalizing here than at standard NSW country tracks.

handicapping_angles.the_hunter_valley_premium: Because Scone is surrounded by elite breeding farms, local trainers (like Paul Messara) often debut highly touted, incredibly well-bred horses here. Always respect first-starters from elite local yards.
handicapping_angles.swooper_friendly: Unlike tight NSW tracks like Canterbury or Wyong, Scone’s 400m straight allows deep closers to build through their gears. Heavily upgrade horses with strong late sectionals; pure front-runners are often run down in the final 50m.
handicapping_angles.carnival_form_translation: During the Scone Cup Carnival (May), the form is effectively Metropolitan grade. Horses that perform well here during the carnival should be given minimal class penalties when shipping to Randwick or Rosehill.
